---
title: "Tolerance calibration for boundary behaviour across GPD fits"
date: "Compiled at `r format(Sys.time(), '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', tz = 'UTC')` UTC"
output:
  github_document: default
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
always_allow_html: true
params:
  name: "01_tol_calibration" # change if you rename file
---

```{r here, message=FALSE}
here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".Rmd"), uuid = "9a024ec6-54e3-4a84-9d54-b965923c1282")
```

Goal: find per-method tolerance so that boundary density matches a consensus.

```{r packages}
library(conflicted)
library(eva)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)

conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter, dplyr::select)
```

```{r directories}
# create or *empty* the target directory, used to write this file's data: 
#projthis::proj_create_dir_target(params$name, clean = TRUE)

# function to get path to target directory: path_target("sample.csv")
path_target <- projthis::proj_path_target(params$name)

# function to get path to previous data: path_source("00-import", "sample.csv")
path_source <- projthis::proj_path_source(params$name)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = c("png", "cairo_pdf"),
  fig.ext = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.width = 10, 
  fig.height = 6, 
  dpi = 200,
  fig.path = paste0('figures/', params, "/tol_calibration_"),
  message = FALSE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  echo = FALSE)
```

## Load permApprox functions

```{r permapprox-load}
# Load permApprox functions
proj_path <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("README.md"))
permApprox_path <- file.path(proj_path, "functions/permApprox_eps_fct/R")
permApprox_functions <- list.files(permApprox_path, pattern = "\\.R$", full.names = TRUE)
invisible(sapply(permApprox_functions, source))
```

## User setting 

Fixed setting: shape = -0.25, scale = 1, boundary = 5

```{r}
set.seed(12345)          # reproducibility
n_rep   <- 100           # number of repetitions
n       <- 100           # sample size per repetition
true_xi <- -0.2          # shape
true_si <- 1             # scale
bound   <- 7             # constraint boundary (eval point)
tols    <- c(1e-5, 1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-9, 1e-10, 1e-11, 1e-12, 1e-13, 1e-14, 1e-15)

# Label helpers
format_tol <- function(x) sub("e-0?", "e-", format(x, scientific = TRUE))
tol_levels <- format_tol(tols)
```

## Method registry

```{r}
# Each entry: name, function, extra args if needed
method_specs <- list(
  list(name = "MLE1D",  fun = .fit_gpd_mle1d,  extra = list()),
  list(name = "MLE2D",  fun = .fit_gpd_mle2d,  extra = list()),
  list(name = "LME",    fun = .fit_gpd_lme,    extra = list()),
  list(name = "NLS2",   fun = .fit_gpd_nls2,   extra = list()),
  list(name = "WNLLSM", fun = .fit_gpd_wnllsm, extra = list())
)
```

## Run simulation

```{r}
# --- One repetition over all methods x tolerances -----------------------------
fit_all_methods <- function(x, boundary, tolerances, methods) {
  map_dfr(methods, function(m) {
    map_dfr(tolerances, function(tol) {
      # robust fit + density extraction
      res <- try({
        do.call(m$fun, c(
          list(x = x, boundary = boundary, eval_point = boundary, tol = tol),
          m$extra
        ))
      }, silent = TRUE)

      if (inherits(res, "try-error") || is.null(res$shape) || is.null(res$scale) ||
          !is.finite(res$shape) || !is.finite(res$scale)) {
        tibble(method = m$name, tol = tol, dgpd = NA_real_)
      } else {
        dens <- eva::dgpd(boundary, scale = res$scale, shape = res$shape)
        tibble(method = m$name, tol = tol, dgpd = dens)
      }
    })
  })
}
```


```{r, eval=FALSE}
# --- Run all repetitions ------------------------------------------------------
draws <- replicate(n_rep, eva::rgpd(n = n, scale = true_si, shape = true_xi), 
                   simplify = FALSE)

res <- map2_dfr(draws, seq_len(n_rep), function(x, r) {
  fit_all_methods(x, boundary = bound, tolerances = tols, 
                  methods = method_specs) %>%
    mutate(rep = r)
})

saveRDS(res, file = path_target("tol_calibration_res_bound7.rds"))
```

## Evaluate results

```{r}
res <- readRDS(path_target("tol_calibration_res_bound7.rds"))

# Clean up factors/labels and log scale
res <- res %>%
  mutate(tol_f = factor(format_tol(tol), levels = tol_levels),
         method = factor(method, 
                         levels = c("MLE1D", "MLE2D", "LME", "NLS2", 
                                    "WNLLSM"))) %>%
  relocate(rep, method, tol_f, dgpd) %>%
  mutate(log_dgpd = log(dgpd)) 

min_log_dgpd <- min(res$log_dgpd[!is.infinite(res$log_dgpd)])

res <- res %>%
  mutate(log_dgpd = ifelse(is.infinite(log_dgpd),
                           min_log_dgpd * 0.001,
                           log_dgpd))
```

### Identify ground truth method

Ground truth per repetition = robust center across tolerances (median of log densities)

```{r}
# For each repetition and method, compute variability across tolerances.
var_by_rep_method <- res %>%
  group_by(rep, method) %>%
  summarise(sd = sd(dgpd, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_log = sd(log_dgpd, na.rm = TRUE),
            mad = mad(dgpd, na.rm = TRUE),
            mad_log = mad(log_dgpd, constant = 1, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

# Aggregate variability across reps
var_summary <- var_by_rep_method %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(#mean_sd  = mean(sd, na.rm = TRUE),
            #median_sd = median(sd, na.rm = TRUE),
            #mean_mad = mean(mad, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_sd_log  = mean(sd_log, na.rm = TRUE),
            median_sd_log = median(sd_log, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_mad_log = mean(mad_log, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_rep = dplyr::n(),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mean_sd_log)

cat("\nWithin-repetition variability (smaller is better):")

var_summary %>%
  kableExtra::kable()

ground_truth_method <- var_summary$method[1]

cat(sprintf("Ground truth method (lowest mean SD on log scale): %s", 
                ground_truth_method))
```


```{r}
# Ground truth per repetition = robust center across tolerances (median of log densities)
gt <- res %>%
  filter(method == ground_truth_method) %>%
  group_by(rep) %>%
  summarise(gt_log = median(log_dgpd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```

### Choose best tolerance per method to match ground truth

For each method and tol, we compute MAE to ground truth across repetitions.

```{r}
# For each method and tol, compute MAE and RMSE to ground truth across repetitions.
fit_vs_gt <- res %>%
  left_join(gt, by = "rep") %>%
  mutate(abs_err = abs(log_dgpd - gt_log)) %>%
  group_by(method, tol_f) %>%
  summarise(MAE_log = mean(abs_err, na.rm = TRUE),
            RMSE_log = sqrt(mean((log_dgpd - gt_log)^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
            n = dplyr::n(),
            .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
# Choose the largest tolerance within a small margin of the minimum MAE
# rel: relative margin (e.g., 0.02 means within +2% of the min MAE)
# abs: absolute margin on the MAE scale (set one of rel or abs; rel is default)
choose_tol_plateau <- function(fit_vs_gt, rel = 0.02, abs = NULL) {
  stopifnot(all(c("method","tol_f","MAE_log") %in% names(fit_vs_gt)))

  df <- fit_vs_gt %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      tol_num = as.numeric(as.character(tol_f))  # "1e-08" -> 1e-08
    ) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(method) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      min_mae = min(MAE_log, na.rm = TRUE),
      thr     = if (!is.null(abs)) min_mae + abs else min_mae * (1 + rel)
    ) %>%
    # keep candidates within the plateau
    dplyr::filter(MAE_log <= thr) %>%
    # pick the LARGEST tolerance within plateau
    dplyr::slice_max(order_by = tol_num, n = 1, with_ties = TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(method, tol_f, MAE_log)

  dplyr::rename(df, tol_f = tol_f)
}
```


```{r}
tol_f_by_method <- choose_tol_plateau(fit_vs_gt, abs = 0.005)

cat("\nBest tol per method vs ground truth (by MAE on log scale):")
tol_f_by_method %>%
  kableExtra::kable()
```


## Plot

We plot repetition vs dgpd (log y), colored by tolerance, shaped by method

### All methods

```{r}
# --- Map chosen tolerance per method -----------------------------------------
tol_f_map <- setNames(tol_f_by_method$tol_f, tol_f_by_method$method)

# choose the first 10 reps for display
res10 <- res |>
  dplyr::filter(rep <= 10)

# define per-method horizontal offsets (tune width as you like)
method_levels <- levels(res10$method)
k <- length(method_levels)
# symmetric offsets, e.g. -0.3, -0.1, 0.1, 0.3 for 4 methods
base <- seq(-(k-1)/2, (k-1)/2, by = 1)
offsets <- 0.15 * base  # control separation width here

offset_map <- setNames(offsets, method_levels)
res10 <- res %>%
  dplyr::filter(rep <= 10,
                tol %in% c(1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-9, 1e-10, 1e-11, 1e-12, 1e-13)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x_plot = rep + offset_map[as.character(method)],
    is_best = tol_f == tol_f_map[as.character(method)]
  )

# Small subtitle string listing choices
choice_txt <- paste0(tol_f_by_method$method, ": ", tol_f_by_method$tol_f) %>%
  paste(collapse = "  |  ")
```

```{r dgpd_vs_rep_all_methods, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
# --- Plot with highlighted chosen tolerances ---------------------------------
p_side_best <- ggplot(res10, aes(x = x_plot, y = dgpd,
                                 color = tol_f, shape = method)) +
  # all points (faded)
  geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.5) +
  #geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  # halo for chosen tol (black underlay, same shapes)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             color = "black", size = 3.6, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # colored chosen tol on top (bigger + opaque)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             size = 2.6, alpha = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "tol") +
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "method") +
  # snap ticks back to integer repetitions
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(res10$rep)), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Repetition (first 10)",
    y = expression(f[GPD](x[eval])),
    title = "Boundary density across tolerances with side-by-side methods",
    subtitle = paste0("Highlighted = chosen tolerance per method:\n", choice_txt)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_side_best)
```

### MLE1D and LME

```{r}
sel_methods <- c("MLE1D", "LME")

# --- Map chosen tolerance per method -----------------------------------------
tol_f_by_method_sel <- tol_f_by_method %>% 
  filter(method %in% sel_methods)

res10 <- res %>%
  dplyr::filter(rep <= 25,
                method %in% sel_methods,
                tol %in% c(1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-9, 1e-10, 1e-11, 1e-12, 1e-13)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x_plot = rep + offset_map[as.character(method)],
    is_best = tol_f == tol_f_map[as.character(method)]
  )

# Small subtitle string listing choices
choice_txt <- paste0(tol_f_by_method_sel$method, ": ",
                     tol_f_by_method_sel$tol_f) %>%
  paste(collapse = "  |  ")
```

```{r dgpd_vs_rep_MLE1D_LME, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
# --- Plot with highlighted chosen tolerances ---------------------------------
p_side_best <- ggplot(res10, aes(x = x_plot, y = dgpd,
                                 color = tol_f, shape = method)) +
  # all points (faded)
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  # halo for chosen tol (black underlay, same shapes)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             color = "black", size = 3.6, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # colored chosen tol on top (bigger + opaque)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             size = 2.6, alpha = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "tol") +
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "method") +
  # snap ticks back to integer repetitions
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(res10$rep)), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Repetition (first 25)",
    y = expression(f[GPD](x[eval])),
    title = "Boundary density across tolerances with side-by-side methods",
    subtitle = paste0("Highlighted = chosen tolerance per method:\n", choice_txt)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_side_best)
```



### MLE1D and NLS2

```{r}
sel_methods <- c("MLE1D", "NLS2")

# --- Map chosen tolerance per method -----------------------------------------
tol_f_by_method_sel <- tol_f_by_method %>% 
  filter(method %in% sel_methods)

res10 <- res %>%
  dplyr::filter(rep <= 25,
                method %in% sel_methods,
                tol %in% c(1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-9, 1e-10, 1e-11, 1e-12, 1e-13)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x_plot = rep + offset_map[as.character(method)],
    is_best = tol_f == tol_f_map[as.character(method)]
  )

# Small subtitle string listing choices
choice_txt <- paste0(tol_f_by_method_sel$method, ": ",
                     tol_f_by_method_sel$tol_f) %>%
  paste(collapse = "  |  ")
```

```{r dgpd_vs_rep_MLE1D_NLS2, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
# --- Plot with highlighted chosen tolerances ---------------------------------
p_side_best <- ggplot(res10, aes(x = x_plot, y = dgpd,
                                 color = tol_f, shape = method)) +
  # all points (faded)
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  # halo for chosen tol (black underlay, same shapes)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             color = "black", size = 3.6, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # colored chosen tol on top (bigger + opaque)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             size = 2.6, alpha = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "tol") +
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "method") +
  # snap ticks back to integer repetitions
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(res10$rep)), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Repetition (first 25)",
    y = expression(f[GPD](x[eval])),
    title = "Boundary density across tolerances with side-by-side methods",
    subtitle = paste0("Highlighted = chosen tolerance per method:\n", choice_txt)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_side_best)
```

### MLE1D and WNLLSM

```{r}
sel_methods <- c("MLE1D", "WNLLSM")

# --- Map chosen tolerance per method -----------------------------------------
tol_f_by_method_sel <- tol_f_by_method %>% 
  filter(method %in% sel_methods)

res10 <- res %>%
  dplyr::filter(rep <= 25,
                method %in% sel_methods,
                tol %in% c(1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-9, 1e-10, 1e-11, 1e-12, 1e-13)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    x_plot = rep + offset_map[as.character(method)],
    is_best = tol_f == tol_f_map[as.character(method)]
  )

# Small subtitle string listing choices
choice_txt <- paste0(tol_f_by_method_sel$method, ": ",
                     tol_f_by_method_sel$tol_f) %>%
  paste(collapse = "  |  ")
```

```{r dgpd_vs_rep_MLE1D_WNLLSM, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
# --- Plot with highlighted chosen tolerances ---------------------------------
p_side_best <- ggplot(res10, aes(x = x_plot, y = dgpd,
                                 color = tol_f, shape = method)) +
  # all points (faded)
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.8) +
  # halo for chosen tol (black underlay, same shapes)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             color = "black", size = 3.6, alpha = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # colored chosen tol on top (bigger + opaque)
  geom_point(data = dplyr::filter(res10, is_best),
             inherit.aes = TRUE,
             size = 2.6, alpha = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = "tol") +
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "method") +
  # snap ticks back to integer repetitions
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(res10$rep)), minor_breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    x = "Repetition (first 25)",
    y = expression(f[GPD](x[eval])),
    title = "Boundary density across tolerances with side-by-side methods",
    subtitle = paste0("Highlighted = chosen tolerance per method:\n", choice_txt)
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_side_best)
```


## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
